perturbation.original_text: (a) "Contract Period" shall mean that period of time commencing on January 1, 2000 and concluding December 31, 2003, unless terminated sooner as provided herein.
perturbation.changed_text: (a) "Contract Period" shall mean that period of time commencing on January 1, 2000 and concluding December 31, 2003, with an option to extend annually upon mutual written consent of both parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines a fixed contract period. By adding 'with an option to extend annually upon mutual written consent of both parties', this contradicts section 11 which outlines specific conditions for termination. Now there's uncertainty: does the contract terminate on December 31, 2003, or can it be extended? This affects enforcement because it's unclear what happens if either party doesn't consent to an extension, creating an in-text contradiction on contract term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) If either party at any time during the Contract Period shall (i) fail to make any payment of any sum of money herein specified to be made, or (ii) fail to observe or&bbsp;perform any of the covenants, agreements or obligations hereunder (other than the payment of money), the nondefaulting party may terminate this Agreement as follows: as to (i) if such payment is not made within ten (10) days after the defaulting party shall have received written notice of such failure to make payment, or as to (ii) if such default is not cured within thirty (30) days after the defaulting party shall have received written notice specifying in reasonable detail the nature of such default. In order to be a sufficient notice hereunder, any such written notice shall specify in detail each item of default and shall specify the provision of this Agreement which applies to each item of default, and shall specify in detail the action the defaulting party is required to take in order to cure each item of default. The termination rights set forth in this section shall not constitute the exclusive remedy of the nondefaulting party hereunder, however, and if default is made by either party hereunder, the other may resort to such other remedies as said party would have been entitled to if this section had been omitted from this Agreement, subject to the terms of this Agreement. Termination under the provisions of this section shall be without prejudice to any rights or claims which the terminating party may otherwise have against the defaulting party, and if Company is the defaulting party, Company shall be responsible for any and all payments due under the terms of this Agreement in addition to other liabilities set forth above.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined a fixed contract period ending on December 31, 2003. The changed text introduces an option for annual extensions, directly conflicting with the fixed term initially established. This contradicts the contradicted text, which outlines termination conditions applicable 'at any time during the Contract Period'. The changed text creates ambiguity regarding the contract's duration, making it unclear whether the termination clause applies after December 31, 2003, if the contract is extended, thus creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (d) "Duval Identification" means the right to use, subject to the provisions hereof, Duval's name, fame, nickname, initials, autograph, voice, video or film portrayals, facsimile signature, photograph, likeness and image or facsimile image, and any other means of endorsement by Duval used in connection with the advertisement and promotion of Company's Products.
perturbation.changed_text: (d) "Duval Identification" means the right to use, subject to the provisions hereof, Duval's name, fame, nickname, initials, autograph, voice, video or film portrayals, facsimile signature, photograph, likeness and image or facsimile image, and any other means of endorsement by Duval used in connection with the advertisement and promotion of Company's Products, unless Duval objects in writing. This objection can occur at any time.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Duval Identification' grants the company broad rights. Adding 'unless Duval objects in writing. This objection can occur at any time' introduces a contradiction. It suggests Duval can unilaterally revoke rights previously granted, creating uncertainty about the scope and duration of the company's rights. This contradiction affects section 2, which grants those rights, since now that grant is conditional and indefinite.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the remuneration to be paid to Licensor pursuant hereto, Licensor grants to Company the right and license during the Contract Period to use the Duval Identification solely in connection with the advertisement and promotion of Company's Products within the Contract Territory as set forth herein. Licensor agrees not to grant the right to use the Duval Identification to anyone other than Company in connection with the advertisement and promotion of Products. It is understood that Company may not use the Duval Identification in connection with any items for sale or resale, other than Company Products as specified herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text grants the company rights to Duval's identification for the contract period. The changed text allows Duval to unilaterally revoke these rights at any time. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states 'Licensor grants to Company the right and license during the Contract Period'. The amendment introduces uncertainty and makes the grant of rights conditional and indefinite, undermining the original agreement and creates a strong, direct conflict regarding the duration and irrevocability of the rights granted.

perturbation.original_text: Company agrees to pay all charges in connection with the delivery of such Products to Duval, including shipping charges, air freight charges and customs charges.
perturbation.changed_text: Company agrees to pay all charges in connection with the delivery of such Products to Duval, including shipping charges, air freight charges and customs charges, unless otherwise agreed upon in writing by Duval.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause in section 8(c) clearly states that the Company will pay all delivery charges for products provided to Duval. By adding 'unless otherwise agreed upon in writing by Duval', it introduces a contradiction and creates uncertainty about who is responsible for these costs. This directly contradicts the prior statement and could lead to disputes over payment obligations. It creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) Licensor agrees to cause Duval to wear Company's Products sold by Company when playing professional golf, and while participating in golf exhibitions and outings, upon the condition that Company supply Duval with such amounts of properly fitting Products, in fabrics and styles approved by Duval and Company, as Duval may reasonably request which are suitable for his use in tournament competition. Company agrees to pay all charges in connection with the delivery of such Products to Duval, including shipping charges, air freight charges and customs charges, unless otherwise agreed upon in writing by Duval. Company agrees to reimburse Licensor's authorized agent for all such expenses incurred by it in connection with the transfer of such Products to Duval.
perturbation.justification: The original clause stated that the Company would pay all delivery charges. The changed text adds "unless otherwise agreed upon in writing by Duval," which introduces an exception to the payment obligation. This creates a direct contradiction within section (c) itself, as it is now unclear whether the Company or Duval is responsible for the delivery charges, violating the Structural Flaws definition and creating uncertainty about the obligation.
